news.summary: The cryptocurrency market saw a 2.5% increase on May 8, pushing its total capitalization above $3 trillion for the first time in over eight weeks. Bitcoin and Ether led the gains, rising around 2.3% and 4%, respectively. The Federal Reserve's decision to maintain interest rates and concerns about stagflation have boosted Bitcoin's appeal as a store of value. Anticipation of a US-UK trade deal and a technical rebound also fueled market optimism. The total market capitalization is currently at $3.06 trillion, with the market seeking to break above the resistance zone between $3.1 trillion and $3.25 trillion to potentially reach all-time highs. The daily RSI suggests bullish momentum is accelerating. Investors are turning to cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin as a hedge against inflation, echoing the trends seen during the 2020 monetary easing period. Trump's administration signals a pro-crypto stance, with reports hinting at an upcoming trade deal announcement with the UK, which could de-escalate global trade tensions and boost risk appetite in the market. The market is showing signs of sustained uptrend, with the $3 trillion mark being a key level to watch for further potential growth.
news.headline: Crypto market up 2.5% on May 8, cap above $3T 1st time in 8 wks. Fed's steady rates, US-UK deal expectations fueling optimism. BTC, ETH up 2.3%, 4% respectively.

news.summary: At the Munich Leaders Meeting in Washington, U.S. Vice President J.D. Vance emphasized the importance of unity between the U.S. and Europe, a significant departure from his previous stance. In contrast to his previous criticism of EU policies on immigration and free speech in February, Vance now expressed his belief that the U.S. and Europe are "on the same team." This shift in perspective was noted by the South China Morning Post. Vance highlighted the deeply rooted connections between the two regions during his speech, signaling a change in his approach towards transatlantic relations. The news was shared during an event that took place on Wednesday, showcasing Vance's altered viewpoint on the matter.
news.headline: JD Vance shifts tone, emphasizes unity between US and Europe at Munich Leaders Meeting. Previous criticism contrasted.

news.summary: In the first quarter of 2025, the company reported an EBITDA of SAR 1.20 billion, marking a 14% year-over-year increase, and a net profit of SAR 533 million, demonstrating an 18% year-over-year growth. These figures showcase the company's strength in its core shipping operations despite challenging market conditions. The positive performance was bolstered by a profit recovery in Integrated Logistics, as well as new earnings generated from Marine Services, contributing to the overall resilience and success of the company.
news.headline: Bahri's Q1 2025 results show EBITDA SAR 1.20 billion (+14% YoY), net profit SAR 533 million (+18% YoY), reflecting growth.

news.summary: The global Edible Oil Market was valued at approximately USD 245.48 Billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 253.67 Billion in 2025. By 2034, it is expected to grow to around USD 362.80 Billion at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of about 4.06% from 2025 to 2034. The market is witnessing a shift towards healthier, sustainable, and premium oil varieties leading companies to broaden their portfolios. In Southeast Asia, sustainable palm oil production is increasing due to stricter regulations and environmental concerns. Companies in Europe are investing in specialty oils that cater to clean-label and health-oriented trends. North American market leaders are focusing on quality and innovation. Factors driving market growth include the growing world population, urbanization, and increasing incomes, leading to higher edible oil consumption. Health consciousness is also driving demand for healthier oil types like olive oil and canola oil. Technological advancements in oil refining and extraction, such as cold pressing and low-chemical refining, are providing nutritional value and environmental sustainability. Key segments analyzed include Product Type, Packaging Type, End-use Analysis, and Region with a comprehensive report scope covering revenue estimation and forecast, competitive landscape, growth factors, recent trends, and regional scope including North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Middle East & Africa, and South & Central America.
news.headline: Global Edible Oil Market expected to reach USD 362.80 Billion by 2034 at 4.06% CAGR, rising from USD 245.48 Billion in 2024. Industry trends focus on healthier, sustainable oils like palm, soybean, sunflower, and rapeseed. Leading companies in Asia, Europe, and North America responding to consumer demand for transparency, health, and sustainability. Increasing world population and health consciousness driving growth, with technology advancements in oil extraction enhancing production efficiencies.

news.summary: In Taipei, Taiwan, United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC) disclosed its unaudited net sales for April 2025. The figures for April 2025 show total revenues of NT$20,454,530 compared to NT$19,741,391 in April 2024, marking a year-over-year increase of NT$713,139, equivalent to a 3.61% rise. For the January to April period in 2025, UMC reported total revenues of NT$78,313,487, up from NT$74,373,490 in the same period in 2024. This represents a year-over-year increase of NT$3,939,997, or a 5.30% rise. All monetary figures are in thousands of New Taiwan Dollars (NT$), while percentages are calculated based on these values. Further details about UMC can be found online at https:/...
news.headline: UMC's April 2025 sales hit NT$20,454,530, up 3.61% Y/Y. Jan.-Apr. revenue reached NT$78,313,487, a 5.30% increase.

news.summary: Contour Design, a leading company specializing in ergonomic computer input devices, has recently disclosed its acquisition of the Active Key business from Cherry SE, a notable move in the industry. Active Key, a reputable entity headquartered in Germany, will now be under Contour Design's umbrella as per the definitive agreement signed. This acquisition marks a significant development in the ergonomic devices sector with the deal finalized on May 8, 2025. The transaction showcases the strategic expansion plans as Contour Design further strengthens its presence in the market.
news.headline: Contour Design acquires Active Key business from Cherry SE, a Germany-based provider, in definitive agreement.

news.summary: Acer Inc. (TWSE: 2353) reported its first quarter financial results for 2025 in Taipei on May 8. The company saw consolidated revenues of NT$61.34 billion, indicating a 4.3% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Gross profits showed growth with margins for both gross profit and operating income expanding compared to the previous year.
news.headline: Acer's Q1'25 operating income rose to NT$1.04B, up 33.0% YoY, with a revenue of NT$61.34B, up 4.3% YoY.

news.summary: In Munich on May 8, 2025, HYXiPOWER made a comeback at Intersolar 2025, the premier global exhibition for the solar sector. The company presented its complete range of products for residential, commercial, industrial (C&I), and utility-scale uses to cater to various needs. This return marked an important moment for HYXiPOWER as it aimed to display its capabilities in powering diverse scenarios at the event.
news.headline: HYXiPOWER showcases its full product lineup at Intersolar 2025 in Munich, ranging from residential to utility-scale applications.

news.summary: In Herlev, Denmark, on May 8, 2025, Nordic Bioscience revealed the launch of the PRO-C3 test by Roche Diagnostics on their cobas analyzers. The Roche Elecsys® PRO-C3, in conjunction with the ADAPT formula which takes into account age, diabetes status, PRO-C3 levels, and platelet count, is now available for assessing liver fibrosis severity. The collaboration offers a significant advancement in liver health monitoring, with the PRO-C3 test providing crucial information that could potentially alter the future of diagnosing and managing liver fibrosis. This development marks a pivotal moment in healthcare as it combines cutting-edge technology with medical expertise to combat a pressing global health issue.
news.headline: Nordic Bioscience's PRO-C3 test is launched by Roche Diagnostics on cobas analysers to assess liver fibrosis severity.

news.summary: In Singapore on May 8, 2025, QDX extends congratulations to A/Prof. Giuseppe Barca, their Co-Founder and Head of Research, for winning the prestigious 2025 Dirac Medal. This award is recognized as the most esteemed international accolade for emerging researchers in the fields of theoretical and computational chemistry. A/Prof. Barca's exceptional work has secured him this honor, highlighting his significant contributions to the scientific community.
news.headline: A/Prof. Giuseppe Barca of QDX receives 2025 Dirac Medal, highest honor for early-career researchers in chemistry.

news.summary: Bitcoin mining has become profitable in 2025, with Bitcoin trading at $95,000 and transaction fees rising post-2024 halving. However, mining with a gaming PC is inefficient and unprofitable compared to ASIC miners due to low performance, high energy costs, and hardware wear-and-tear. ASICs dominate the mining scene, producing trillions of hashes per second compared to a few hundred megahashes per second from GPUs. Mining Bitcoin with a high-end GPU like the RTX 4090 results in high power consumption without significant earnings. After Sept. 30, 2025, 4GB GPUs will no longer work due to DAG size limits. According to WhatToMine, alternative cryptocurrencies like Ethereum Classic (ETC), Ravencoin (RVN), and Monero (XMR) are more GPU-friendly options for mining on gaming PCs in 2025.
news.headline: Bitcoin mining is enticing as BTC hits $95,000 in May 2025. Gaming PCs can mine, but ASICs dominate due to efficiency. Opt for altcoins like Ethereum Classic, Ravencoin, Monero for GPU mining.

news.summary: A recent report revealed that the global number of COVID-19 cases has surpassed 150 million, with over 3.1 million deaths recorded. The United States has the highest number of cases, with over 32 million infections and 574,000 deaths. India follows closely with more than 18 million cases and over 200,000 deaths. The situation remains critical in many countries, as efforts to vaccinate populations continue. The World Health Organization has emphasized the importance of following safety measures to curb the spread of the virus.
news.headline: Unaudited Q1 2025 financial data released by Galaxy Entertainment Group.

news.summary: NielsenIQ (NIQ) recently unveiled its global expansion plans for NIQ Product Insights (NPI), a solution aimed at offering comprehensive information on product attributes that consumers seek when shopping. NPI, already popular in the U.S. with top retailers and CPG manufacturers, is set to be launched in 25 more countries. This market-leading solution provides detailed insights into dietary preferences, sustainability factors, and clean label characteristics that influence consumer purchasing decisions.
news.headline: NielsenIQ expands NIQ Product Insights globally, offering CPG & retail enterprises detailed product attribute catalogue.

news.summary: Xsolla, a prominent company in video game commerce, has recently acquired Ludo, a platform known for enhancing community engagement through code-free social quests. This acquisition is a strategic move that brings Ludo's advanced technology under Xsolla's umbrella. By integrating Ludo's questing tools with Xsolla's Web Shop and other Rewards Solutions, developers worldwide can leverage these offerings to boost player engagement, enhance retention rates, and explore new monetization possibilities. This development marks a significant step in Xsolla's efforts to expand its services and strengthen its position in the industry.
news.headline: Xsolla acquires Ludo for player engagement; Ludo's tech to boost Xsolla's portfolio; enhance player retention and monetization.

news.summary: Astronomer has recently experienced significant growth with the launch of Apache Airflow® 3 and increased funding, prompting the company to expand its operations in EMEA. This expansion comes following a surge in customer interest and support for their flagship product, Astro, the premier DataOps platform leveraging the capabilities of Apache Airflow®. The developments have been observed in London on May 8, 2025.
news.headline: Astronomer opens new London office due to high demand in EMEA following release of Apache Airflow® 3; London, May 8, 2025.

news.summary: London, May 8, 2025 - Tech entrepreneur Tim Stokely, known for founding OnlyFans, has introduced Subs.com, a platform geared towards creators that prioritizes brand-friendliness and community-building. The platform aims to empower all creators and help them increase their subscriber count, tapping into a global market.
news.headline: OnlyFans founder Tim Stokely launches Subs.com, a creator-first platform; aims to empower all creators to 'Get More Subs'.

news.summary: In London on May 8, 2025, Cadent, the leading gas distribution network in the UK and a member of GD4S, announced its partnership with OverIT as their strategic Field Service Management (FSM) software provider. The implementation of OverIT's NextGen Platform will assist 3,600 users, which includes field engineers and other staff members.
news.headline: Cadent partners with OverIT for FSM platform to benefit 3,600 users in the UK's gas distribution network.

news.summary: The first patient has been administered with a combination of roginolisib and dostarlimab, with or without docetaxel, in a study for patients with non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who have advanced after previous immunotherapy/chemotherapy treatment. The study aims to assess the safety of the combination and investigate if directing PI3Kδ along with immunotherapy could potentially regulate the immune system. This study could offer valuable insights into the efficacy of this combination therapy for NSCLC patients who have not responded to prior treatments.
news.headline: iOnctura begins Phase I/II trial for NSCLC with roginolisib + dostarlimab +/- docetaxel. Evaluate safety and impact on immune system in patients.
